task:
Fix ticket 0054's remaining defect-class owner: the FAILED-run terminal path leaves the on-disk deb-status.json feed stale (watch.active:true, missing deb-watch-stopped). The wrap/hold/stop paths are already fixed and tested; the fail() funnel is the one terminal path still broken.

FILE 1 — packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, the fail() funnel (currently ~lines 631-647).
The bug: fail() records the terminal `run-end` event (~635-639) then calls `await refreshStatus('faulted', atomIndex, null, ...)` at ~640 — but it does NOT stop the deb watcher first. stopDebWatcher() only runs later in the outer catch (~1494) and there is no refreshStatus after it, so the FINAL on-disk deb-status.json written for a failed run is the one from line 640, computed while the watcher is still 'active' and before the `deb-watch-stopped` event exists. Result: failed runs leave watch.active:true and omit the terminal `deb-watch-stopped` marker — the exact 0054 staleness, on the failed path.
The fix: call `await stopDebWatcher()` AFTER recording the `run-end` event and BEFORE the `await refreshStatus('faulted', ...)` call, mirroring the normal completion path (runner.ts ~1528 stopDebWatcher then ~1551 refreshStatus) and the stop/hold paths. After this, the line-640 refresh will observe the recorded `deb-watch-stopped` event, so renderDebStatus computes watch.active:false and includes deb-watch-stopped + run-end in recentEvents. stopDebWatcher is idempotent (it sets a flag, wakes the sleep, and awaits an already-resolved promise), so the second call in the outer catch remains a harmless no-op. Do NOT change refreshStatus, stopDebWatcher, renderDebStatus, or any other terminal path — only insert the stopDebWatcher() call in fail() at the correct position.

FILE 2 — packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts: add a regression test for the FAILED terminal path, in the same style as the existing WS1/0054 terminal-feed assertions. Model it on the completed-run test near line ~3179-3240 (which asserts finalStatus.watch.active === false, recentEvents includes ['deb-watch-stopped','run-end'], and the last 'deb-status' event index is after the 'run-end' index) and the held/stopped tests at ~3266/3298 (which compare projectionFields(terminal) to the deriveTerminalProjection canonical). Drive a run that funnels into fail() (an existing failed-run test around lines ~1954-2011 shows how to provoke a fault that ends with run-end status:'failed'). Assert on the final on-disk DebStatus (statusWrites.at(-1)) that: (a) watch.active === false, (b) recentEvents' types contain both 'deb-watch-stopped' and 'run-end', (c) the last 'deb-status' event was recorded AFTER the 'run-end' event, and (d) projectionFields(terminal) equals projectionFields of the deriveTerminalProjection-derived canonical render (oscar 'blocked', phase 'faulted'). The test MUST fail without the FILE 1 change and pass with it.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- `pnpm --dir packages/core test` (or the repo's test command for packages/core) passes, including the existing WS1/0054 completed/held/stopped terminal-feed tests and the deriveTerminalProjection suite in status.test.ts — do not weaken or edit those.
- typecheck clean.
- No change to any non-failed terminal path; the only runtime change is the single stopDebWatcher() call inside fail().
- Do not touch files outside packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts and packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts.

Report the exact diff and the test command output (showing the new failed-path test passing and the suite green).
